chapter_title.ar: بَابُ صَيْدِ الْحَرَمِ وَ مَا تَجِبُ فِيهِ الْكَفَّارَةُ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 21 – Hunt of the Al-Harram and what expiation is Obligated with regards to it

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/89)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/89)
text: سَأَلْتُهُ عَنْ قَوْمٍ قَفَّلُوا عَلَى طَائِرٍ مِنْ حَمَامِ الْحَرَمِ الْبَابَ فَمَاتَ قَالَ عَلَيْهِمْ بِقِيمَةِ كُلِّ طَيْرٍ [نِصْفُ] دِرْهَمٍ يُعْلَفُ بِهِ حَمَامُ الْحَرَمِ .

en.hubeali: Safwan Bin yahya, from Ziyad Abu Al Hassan Al Wasity,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Ibrahim<sup>asws</sup>, said, ‘I asked him<sup>asws</sup> about a group locked the door upon birds from the doves of the Al-Harram, so they died. He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Upon them is the price of each bird, half a Dirham, feeding by it the doves of the Al-Harram’.
en.sarwar: 13. From Safwan ibn Yahya has narrated from Ziyad abu al-Hassan al-Wasitiy who has said the following: “I once asked abu Ibrahim, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about the case of a people who lock a door on pigeons of al-Haram (the sacred area) and they die. He (the Imam) said, ‘They are required to pay the price of every bird which is one [half] dirham with which pigeons of al-Haram (the sacred area) are fed.’”
